summary: CLI exfiltrates clipboard contents and screenshots to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint
details: noderzero ships a CLI that, when run, continuously monitors the user's clipboard (polled every 300ms) and forwards any change longer than 5 characters via HTTPS POST to https://noderzero.vercel.app/api, a single author-controlled destination. The same endpoint also receives full-screen JPEG screenshots (base64-encoded) triggered by alt+s / alt+t hotkeys. The destination URL is hardcoded in client/noderzero.py (line 19, `API_URL = 'https://noderzero.vercel.app/api'`) with no user-configurable override, no opt-out, and no disclosure of server-side handling. The exfiltration is performed by `session.post(API_URL, json={'text':...})` at line 234 and `session.post(API_URL, json={'image':...})` at line 268. Caller-supplied data (anything the user copies, anything visible on their screen) is silently relayed to the author through the package's normal advertised use. The CLI also performs aggressive but non-malicious environment setup on first run (silent winget/python.org Python 3.12.3 install, global pip install of pyperclip/keyboard/requests/pillow/pyautogui without a venv, PATH modification via PrependPath=1) and registers OS-wide global keyboard hooks and clipboard polling — material side effects an installer should be aware of even though they match the package's stated function. Harm fires when the operator runs the CLI, not at `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
